title: AI Agent Risk, Oversight, and Failure
modules: The Failure Landscape: How Autonomous Agents Go Wrong
Prompt Injection, Goal Misgeneralization, and Emergent Behavior
Designing Human-in-the-Loop Controls for Agentic Systems
Monitoring, Logging, and Detecting Drift in Production Agents
Risk Frameworks: Evaluating Agents Before Deployment
Case Studies: High-Profile Agent Failures and What They Cost
Apply It: Conduct a Risk Audit of an Agent in Your Organization
synopsis: Deploying AI agents without a structured risk and oversight model is how organizations create expensive, embarrassing, or dangerous failures â often without seeing them coming. This course equips managers, architects, and domain experts with the frameworks to evaluate agent risk, design meaningful oversight checkpoints, and build accountable AI systems.
rationale: As agentic AI deployment accelerates, the professionals responsible for oversight and governance urgently need structured mental models for agent failure modes that go well beyond standard AI ethics talking points.
learning_outcome: Conduct a structured risk audit of an AI agent system â identifying failure vectors, assigning accountability, and specifying actionable oversight controls before or after deployment.
